build_system: Build the managed-only tracer with `./tracer/build.sh` (Linux/macOS) or `.\tracer\build.cmd` (Windows); Nuke coordinates .NET builds plus CMake/vcpkg for native components. Run unit tests via `./tracer/build.sh BuildAndRunManagedUnitTests` and integration tests via `./tracer/build.sh BuildAndRunIntegrationTests`. For Linux native profiler/loader/tracer work, build inside the Alpine builder image via `./tracer/build_in_docker.sh` (direct cmake does not work) — do a cold build with `BuildTracerHome BuildNativeLoader BuildNativeWrapper BuildProfilerHome BuildProfilerSamples`, then iterate with just `BuildProfilerHome` after profiler-only edits (use `BuildNativeLoader`, `BuildTracerHome`, or `BuildNativeWrapper` for their respective source areas). Use `./tracer/build_in_docker.sh RunProfilerScenario --scenario <id> --scenario-timeout <s>` to run a sample app with the freshly built profiler attached; logs and `.pprof` files land under `artifacts/build_data/profiler-scenario/`. All CI setup steps use `actions/setup-dotnet` with `global-json-file: global.json`, so confirm the SDK version with `dotnet --version` against `global.json`. Windows CI jobs enable long paths via `git config --system core.longpaths true` before checkout.
test_infrastructure: Unit tests use xUnit (managed) and GoogleTest (native); write assertions inline with FluentAssertions style (`SomeMethod().Should().Be(expected)`). Many integration tests require Docker services defined in `docker-compose.yml`. Nuke exposes `BuildAndRunManagedUnitTests` and `BuildAndRunIntegrationTests` targets for local/CI use. CI also runs several "verify generated output" jobs on `pull_request` that rebuild generated artifacts and fail if `git diff` shows uncommitted drift: `verify_source_generated_changes_are_persisted.yml` (Generated folders + `supported_calltargets.g.json` via `Restore CompileManagedSrc`), `verify_solution_changes_are_persisted.yml` (`RegenerateSolutions`), `verify_app_trimming_changes_are_persisted.yml` (`Datadog.Trace.Trimming.xml` via `CreateTrimmingFile`), `verify_files_without_nullability.yml` (`missing-nullability-files.csv` via `CreateMissingNullabilityFile`), `verify_span_metadata_markdown_is_updated.yml` (`docs/span_metadata.md` via `GenerateSpanDocumentation`), and `verify_generated_pipeline_is_updated.yml` (`.azure-pipelines/steps/update-github-pipeline-status.yml` via `GenerateUpdateGitHubPipelineStep`). `verify_integrations_map_added.yml` runs `GeneratePackageVersions` and, on failure, instructs updating `tracer/build/_build/Honeypot/IntegrationGroups.cs` for new integrations. CodeQL analysis (csharp, cpp) exists in `.github/workflows/codeql-analysis.yml` but its jobs are currently disabled (`if: false`).
sharp_edges: Never manually edit generated files (containing `.g.` in the extension, or under `Generated` folders, `supported_calltargets.g.json`, `Datadog.Trace.Trimming.xml`, `missing-nullability-files.csv`, `docs/span_metadata.md`, or generated solution files) — regenerate them with the corresponding Nuke target and commit the output, since dedicated CI jobs diff and fail the PR otherwise. Adding a new integration requires updating `tracer/build/_build/Honeypot/IntegrationGroups.cs` (`IntegrationGroup.IntegrationMap`) or `verify_integrations_map_added.yml` fails. Adding a new sample/solution requires running `RegenerateSolutions` locally and committing the diff. On Windows CI/scripts, enable `git config --system core.longpaths true` before other steps to avoid path-length failures. The `Datadog.Trace` NuGet package ships only manual instrumentation (`Datadog.Trace.Manual.dll`); the full auto-instrumentation `Datadog.Trace.dll` and native profiler binaries are distributed separately via the monitoring home, not the NuGet package. Do not use `ToString()` on numeric types in log calls (use the generic `Log.Debug<int>` style overloads to avoid allocations), and avoid string interpolation in log calls in favor of format strings. Terminology in customer-facing logs matters: use "Datadog SDK", "Instrumentation"/"Instrumentation component", "Continuous Profiler", and "Datadog.Trace.dll" rather than ambiguous terms like "profiler" or "managed profiler".
pr_ci_rules: The PR template (`.github/pull_request_template.md`) has sections: Summary of changes, Reason for change, Implementation details, Test coverage, Other details (with a `Fixes #{issue}` placeholder); populate this template rather than inventing headers. Where possible obtain 2 approvals before merging (1 review from apm-dotnet plus 1 from the external team is typical for external contributions; trivial changes need not have 2). MergeQueue is not enabled — with write access, 1-2 approvals, and passing required checks, use Squash and Merge. A PR carrying the `status:do-not-merge` label is force-failed by `do_not_merge.yml`. `auto_code_freeze_block_pr.yml` sets a `code_freeze` commit status to failure whenever a milestone titled "Code Freeze" is open, blocking merges into `main`/`master`/`release/**`/`hotfix/**` during freezes; version-bump/bot PRs can have these checks force-overridden via the `override_version_bump_pr_checks.yml` workflow (only valid on `version-bump-*` or `bot/*` branches). `auto_label_prs.yml` auto-assigns labels and validates that a PR has labels on open/synchronize/reopen. `auto_add_vnext_milestone_to_pr.yml` assigns merged PRs (except `[Version Bump]` PRs) to the vNext milestone. `auto_check_snapshots.yml` posts a snapshot-diff summary comment on PRs. Docker images built for a PR (labeled `docker_image_artifacts`) are deleted from GHCR when the PR closes.
file_layout: Managed tracer source lives under `tracer/src/Datadog.Trace` (subfolders per concern: `ClrProfiler` for auto-instrumentation/CallTarget, `Configuration`, `Debugger`, `AppSec`, `Ci`, `ContinuousProfiler`, `DuckTyping`, `Propagators`, `Telemetry`, etc.); other tracer modules (Manual API, Loader, source generators, OpenTracing bridge, MSBuild tasks, CLI tools, trimming, Azure Functions, FleetInstaller) sit alongside it under `tracer/src`. Native interop glue is in `Datadog.Tracer.Native`; native profiler/tests are under `profiler/src` and `profiler/test`; cross-cutting native libs live in `shared`. Tests live under `tracer/test` (unit/integration) with sample apps in `tracer/test/test-applications`; native profiler tests are under `profiler/test`. Solutions for IDE use are `Datadog.Trace.sln` and `Datadog.Profiler.sln`. GitHub automation lives under `.github/workflows` (release, version-bump, code-freeze, and generated-file verification workflows) and `.github/pull_request_template.md`; Azure DevOps pipeline definitions live under `.azure-pipelines`. Documentation is under `docs`, including `docs/development/*` guides and `docs/span_metadata.md` (generated).
